feedback: The analysis is strong on the rate-limit retry problem and identifies the right modules, configuration, and broad test areas, but it does not fully resolve the second requirement: plan and develop processes must run in feature/<issue_id>, while the proposed criteria only ensure the branch exists. That leaves a core behavioral ambiguity unaddressed, especially for develop, where committing or checking out the correct branch may be required rather than merely calling ensure_feature_branch().
missing_sections: Precise branch workflow for plan and develop
Definition of done for develop branch behavior
concerns: Calling ensure_feature_branch(issue_iid) does not prove the plan or develop process actually runs on feature/<issue_id>.
The develop requirement is left as an open question, but it is central to the original issue and must be resolved before approval.
Acceptance criteria for rate-limit detection depend on vague keyword matching rather than confirmed Claude/Codex error outputs.
The proposed retry wrapper catches ClaudeCodeError/CodexError, but it is unclear whether all six wrappers raise those errors consistently with enough stdout/stderr context.
CI timeout risk is identified but not converted into a verifiable requirement.
suggestions: Add explicit acceptance criteria that verify the working tree or subprocess context is on feature/<issue_id> for both plan and develop.
Clarify whether develop should commit implementation changes to feature/<issue_id> instead of develop/<issue_id>.
Add tests that assert checkout/branch selection behavior, not only ensure_feature_branch() invocation.
Mock sleep in retry tests and verify retry count, delay configuration, final failure behavior, and logging.
Define confirmed rate-limit fixtures for Claude and Codex before implementation or make patterns configurable from the start.
